game_name: Might and Magic VI: The Mandate of Heaven

danger_signals.visual_form: First-person view transition to combat rendering, enemy sprite manifestation in dungeon/wilderness
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat present; immediate tactical decision required

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental darkness, sky color shift to nocturnal palette
danger_signals.meaning: Temporal state change affecting entity availability and behavior patterns

danger_signals.visual_form: Underground architecture, confined spaces, narrow corridors
danger_signals.meaning: High-danger zone with concentrated enemy density and limited retreat options

danger_signals.visual_form: Character portrait/status indicator showing reduced vitality metric
danger_signals.meaning: Party vulnerability state; escalating threat to continued viability

safety_signals.visual_form: Architectural transition to humanoid structures, NPCs present, calm environment lighting
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for rest, resource acquisition, quest completion

safety_signals.visual_form: Environmental brightness, sky color shift to diurnal palette
safety_signals.meaning: Reduced danger period; certain events unavailable

safety_signals.visual_form: Specific building type within settlement, NPC trainer availability
safety_signals.meaning: Character advancement opportunity; safe progression mechanic

safety_signals.visual_form: Interactable environmental object with distinctive appearance
safety_signals.meaning: Stat improvement opportunity; resource benefit without combat

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Town guards (allied), hostile humanoid NPCs (hostile faction members)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Structured architecture, guild halls, training centers, quest-givers clustered in populated areas

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinctive creature models per enemy class (beasts, undead, magical constructs differentiate through sprite/model design)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Wilderness zones, dungeons, unstructured terrain indicate monster territory; specific biome types correlate to creature faction presence

faction_indicators.faction: Calendar-based event entities (Circus of the Sun, Solstice phenomena)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Temporary NPC manifestation with unique sprite set during scheduled windows
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Temporal markers (calendar day/season state) trigger visual presence of faction entities in specific locations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bright environmental lighting, sky palette shift to daylight colors, specific NPCs visible in settlements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Certain scheduled events unavailable (e.g., Circus of the Sun appears only at specific times); NPC availability changes; some monsters may have altered spawn rates or behavior patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark environmental lighting, sky palette shift to nocturnal colors, reduced NPC visibility in outdoor areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Alternative event scheduling becomes active; dungeon exploration becomes primary activity path; potentially increased monster activity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Potentially unique environmental effects or NPC manifestations tied to seasonal transition
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Special events triggered; unique quest opportunities or entity encounters available only during this world state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character portrait/status display shows age metric; visual aging effects on character representation beyond age 50
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stat penalties applied once character age exceeds 50; affects combat viability and party composition strategy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health point indicators on character portraits/status display show reduced values; visual damage indicators on party member representations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Tactical decision-making shifts toward survival (retreat, healing priority); enemy aggression patterns may intensify; threat level perception increases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Deceased character removed from party visual formation; status display marks character as deceased/unavailable
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party combat capability reduced; specific skills/magic dependent on that class become unavailable; resurrection quest becomes priority

environmental_storytelling.example: Settlement structures communicate civilization presence and safety; dungeon layouts communicate danger escalation through density and complexity
Specific enemy types appear in correlated terrain types; player learns to associate visual environment with threat level and creature type
Explorable vertical space signals progression and discovery opportunity; visual elevation change communicates world geography and accessible vs. restricted areas
Barrels, cauldrons, shrines, wells, fountains scattered in world communicate stat improvement opportunities; their presence suggests resource abundance or intentional waypoint placement
Specific building type signals skill acquisition opportunity; architectural clustering communicates functional settlement zones
Circus of the Sun manifestation signals time passage and world dynamism; temporal visual markers communicate passage of in-game time to player

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Full stat functionality; no age-based penalties; optimal combat and exploration viability
Stat penalties applied; reduced combat effectiveness; visual aging indicator present in character status display
Can accept single promotion quest; increased stat/capability benefits upon successful completion; class title changes
Can pursue second promotion quest; two promotions maximum per class; non-promoted classes receive honorary promotion option
Present in settlement; available for interaction and quest acceptance
Potentially unavailable or relocated; reduces quest availability depending on NPC schedule
Absent from world; no visual manifestation; not available for interaction
Manifests in specific location; available for interaction; unique visual appearance signaling special event status
Spawn rates potentially reduced; behavioral patterns may shift toward less aggressive engagement
Spawn rates potentially increased; dungeon exploration becomes primary threat vector; behavioral patterns may shift toward increased aggression

notes: Might and Magic VI communicates world-state primarily through temporal systems (day/night, calendar scheduling) and character life-cycle mechanics (aging, promotion status). Danger and safety are signaled through environment type (settlement vs. wilderness/dungeon), NPC presence, and lighting conditions. Faction identity is minimal in available documentation but implied through creature type clustering and NPC settlement organization. The game uses first-person perspective to make environmental visual communication direct and immersive. Stat modification through environmental interactions (barrels, shrines, etc.) creates a visual-spatial language for player progression without explicit UI. Character aging as a visible mechanic communicates temporal passage and creates strategic resource management pressure.
